name.text: Week 10 – Q5: Parallel RLC Natural Response (Four-Switch Circuit)

questiontext.text:
Week 10 – Q5: Parallel RLC Natural Response (Four-Switch Circuit)

Course: Electromagnetism and Circuit Analysis — LUT University, Finland

This is a training exercise — you may use books, tutors, and AI. The question requires multi-step reasoning.

Total: 8 points | Penalty per wrong attempt: 10%

The circuit below has four switches (SW1–SW4) that all change state simultaneously at \(t = 0\):

  • SW1 (between \(R_a\) and \(L\)): closed for \(t < 0\), opens at \(t = 0\).
  • SW2 (between \(L\) and \(R\)): open for \(t < 0\), closes at \(t = 0\).
  • SW3 (between \(R\) and \(C\)): open for \(t < 0\), closes at \(t = 0\).
  • SW4 (between \(C\) and \(R_b\)): closed for \(t < 0\), opens at \(t = 0\).

The switches have been in their \(t < 0\) positions for a long time (DC steady state).

At \(t = 0\), the switches change state. Find \(v_o(t)\) for \(t \geq 0\).

Circuit parameters:

  • Current source: \(I_s = {@Is_val@}\;\text{A}\)
  • Left-side resistor: \(R_a = {@Ra_val@}\;\Omega\)
  • Inductor: \(L = {@L_mH@}\;\text{mH}\)
  • Parallel resistor: \(R = {@R_val@}\;\Omega\)
  • Right-side resistor: \(R_b = {@Rb_val@}\;\Omega\)
  • DC voltage source: \(V_{dc} = {@Vdc_val@}\;\text{V}\)
  • Capacitor: \(C = {@C_uF@}\;\mu\text{F}\)

Part (a) — Initial Capacitor Voltage \(V_0\) (1 pt)

Before the switches change state (\(t < 0\)), the circuit has been in DC steady state for a long time. At DC steady state, the capacitor is an open circuit (no current flows through it or \(R_b\)). What is the initial capacitor voltage \(V_0 = v_o(0^-)\) in Volts?

\(V_0 = \) [[input:ans1]] V [[validation:ans1]]

Syntax hint: Enter a number, e.g. 100

Part (b) — Initial Inductor Current \(I_0\) (1 pt)

At DC steady state (\(t < 0\)), the inductor is a short circuit. What is the initial inductor current \(I_0 = i_L(0^-)\) in Amperes?

\(I_0 = \) [[input:ans2]] A [[validation:ans2]]

Syntax hint: Enter a number, e.g. 5

Part (c) — Damping Coefficient \(\alpha\) (1 pt)

After the switches change state (\(t > 0\)), the sources are disconnected and the circuit becomes a source-free parallel RLC circuit with \(R = {@R_val@}\;\Omega\), \(L = {@L_mH@}\;\text{mH}\), and \(C = {@C_uF@}\;\mu\text{F}\).

Calculate the damping coefficient \(\alpha = \frac{1}{2RC}\) in rad/s.

\(\alpha = \) [[input:ans3]] rad/s [[validation:ans3]]

Syntax hint: Enter a number, e.g. 1000

Part (d) — Resonant Frequency \(\omega_0\) (1 pt)

Calculate the undamped resonant frequency \(\omega_0 = \frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}}\) in rad/s.

\(\omega_0 = \) [[input:ans4]] rad/s [[validation:ans4]]

Syntax hint: Enter a number, e.g. 1000

Part (e) — Damping Regime Classification (1 pt)

Compare \(\alpha^2\) with \(\omega_0^2\) and classify the damping regime:

Select your answer: [[input:ans5]] [[validation:ans5]]

Part (f) — General Response Form (1 pt)

Write the general form of \(v_o(t)\) for \(t \geq 0\), appropriate to the damping regime you identified. Use symbolic constants (A1, A2 for overdamped; B1, B2 for underdamped; D1, D2 for critically damped).

Note: This is a natural response (source-free circuit), so there is no forced (steady-state) term.

\(v_o(t) = \) [[input:ans6]] [[validation:ans6]]

Syntax hint: Use exp(...), sin(...), cos(...), and t. Example: A1*exp(-20.9*t) + A2*exp(-479.1*t)

Part (g) — Complete \(v_o(t)\) with Numerical Coefficients (1 pt)

Apply the initial conditions \(v_o(0^+) = V_0 = {@V0_val@}\;\text{V}\) and \(\frac{dv_o}{dt}(0^+) = -\frac{1}{C}\!\left(\frac{V_0}{R} + I_0\right) = {@dvdt0@}\;\text{V/s}\) to find all coefficients numerically.

Enter the complete expression for \(v_o(t)\) with all numerical values substituted.

\(v_o(t) = \) [[input:ans7]] V [[validation:ans7]]

Syntax hint: Use exp(...), sin(...), cos(...), and t. Example: 100*exp(-400*t)*cos(300*t) - 800*exp(-400*t)*sin(300*t)

Part (h) — Final Value Verification (1 pt)

What is \(\lim_{t \to \infty} v_o(t)\)?

Read off the steady-state value directly from your \(v_o(t)\) expression — the constant term that remains as all exponentials decay to zero.

\(\lim_{t \to \infty} v_o(t) = \) [[input:ans8]] V [[validation:ans8]]

Syntax hint: Enter a number, e.g. 0

generalfeedback.text:
Worked Solution

Step 1: Initial Conditions (DC Steady State for \(t < 0\))

With SW1 and SW4 closed (SW2 and SW3 open) for a long time:

  • Capacitor voltage: At DC steady state, the capacitor is an open circuit. No current flows through \(R_b\), so \(V_0 = V_{dc} = {@V0_val@}\;\text{V}\).
  • Inductor current: At DC steady state, the inductor is a short circuit. All of \(I_s\) flows through the inductor: \(I_0 = I_s = {@I0_val@}\;\text{A}\).

Step 2: Circuit for \(t > 0\)

After the switches change state (SW1, SW4 open; SW2, SW3 close), the sources are disconnected. The remaining circuit is a source-free parallel RLC: \(R = {@R_val@}\;\Omega\), \(L = {@L_val@}\;\text{H}\), \(C = {@C_val@}\;\text{F}\).

Step 3: Damping Parameters

\[\alpha = \frac{1}{2RC} = \frac{1}{2 \times {@R_val@} \times {@C_val@}} = {@ta_alpha@}\;\text{rad/s}\]

\[\omega_0 = \frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{{@L_val@} \times {@C_val@}}} = {@ta_omega0@}\;\text{rad/s}\]

Step 4: Damping Regime

Comparing \(\alpha = {@ta_alpha@}\) with \(\omega_0 = {@ta_omega0@}\):

The circuit is {@regime_name@}.

Step 5: General Form and Coefficients

For a source-free (natural response) parallel RLC, the final value is zero.

General form: \(v_o(t) = {@ta6@}\)

Applying initial conditions:

  • \(v_o(0^+) = V_0 = {@V0_val@}\;\text{V}\)
  • \(\frac{dv_o}{dt}(0^+) = -\frac{1}{C}\!\left(\frac{V_0}{R} + I_0\right) = {@dvdt0@}\;\text{V/s}\)

Solving the system of two equations yields the complete response:

\[v_o(t) = {@ta_vo@}\;\text{V}, \quad t \geq 0\]

Step 6: Final Value

As \(t \to \infty\), all exponential terms decay to zero. Since there is no source in the circuit for \(t > 0\), the final value is:

\[\lim_{t \to \infty} v_o(t) = 0\;\text{V}\]

Answer Summary

PartQuantityCorrect Answer
(a)\(V_0\){@ta1@} V
(b)\(I_0\){@ta2@} A
(c)\(\alpha\){@ta3@} rad/s
(d)\(\omega_0\){@ta4@} rad/s
(e)Damping regime{@regime_name@}
(f)General form\({@ta6@}\)
(g)\(v_o(t)\)\({@ta_vo@}\) V
(h)Final value{@ta8@} V

specificfeedback.text:
Part (a) — Initial Voltage \(V_0\)

[[feedback:prt1]]

Part (b) — Initial Current \(I_0\)

[[feedback:prt2]]

Part (c) — Damping Coefficient \(\alpha\)

[[feedback:prt3]]

Part (d) — Resonant Frequency \(\omega_0\)

[[feedback:prt4]]

Part (e) — Damping Regime

[[feedback:prt5]]

Part (f) — General Response Form

[[feedback:prt6]]

Part (g) — Complete \(v_o(t)\)

[[feedback:prt7]]

Part (h) — Final Value

[[feedback:prt8]]

questionnote.text: Set {@set@} | R={@R_val@}, L={@L_mH@}mH, C={@C_uF@}uF | {@regime_name@} | V0={@V0_val@}V, I0={@I0_val@}A

questiondescription.text: Parallel RLC Natural Response with four switches (SW1–SW4) — find initial conditions and v_o(t). Based on Nilsson P8.11. Week 10 training exercise.

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done!

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.

falsefeedback.text: Incorrect. At DC steady state: capacitor = open circuit, no current through \(R_b\), so \(V_0 = V_{dc} = {@ta1@}\;\text{V}\).
Incorrect. At DC steady state: inductor = short circuit, all source current flows through it: \(I_0 = I_s = {@ta2@}\;\text{A}\).

truefeedback.text: Correct! Your expression evaluates correctly at multiple test points.

node.description: Check final value = 0 — NumAbsolute because answer is 0

falsefeedback.text: Incorrect. No source for \(t > 0\), so all energy dissipates: \(\lim_{t \to \infty} v_o(t) = 0\;\text{V}\).

hint.text: Hint 1 (Pre-switch Analysis): At DC steady state: inductor = short circuit, capacitor = open circuit. Use these to find \(V_0\) and \(I_0\). After switches change state, sources disconnect — source-free parallel RLC remains.

Hint 2 (Formulas): For parallel RLC natural response: \(\alpha = \frac{1}{2RC}\), \(\omega_0 = \frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}}\). Initial rate of change: \(\frac{dv_o}{dt}(0^+) = -\frac{1}{C}\!\left(\frac{V_0}{R} + I_0\right)\).

Hint 3 (Worked Step): For underdamped: \(v_o(t) = e^{-\alpha t}(B_1 \cos\omega_d t + B_2 \sin\omega_d t)\). From \(v_o(0^+) = B_1 = V_0\). Then: \(\frac{dv_o}{dt}(0^+) = -\alpha B_1 + \omega_d B_2\). Solve for \(B_2\).
